data.description:
## Why
Every gate row and all post-ship dogfooding in epic pastoral-aquatic-viper needs standing outcome instrumentation: today nothing routinely ties review verdicts to downstream outcomes (post-claim edits, reopens, force-closes, verifier FAILs), so FP drift is invisible until it burns trust (Tricorder <10% cliff).

## What
Extend docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/ into standing gate-eval instrumentation: a committed job that classifies each reviewed ticket's outcome from store history (the E1 outcome-corpus method, re-runnable on the live store) and emits the trailing FP-proxy metrics the epic's dogfood monitoring uses (per-criterion advisory-application rate, blocking-FP proxy, rounds/ticket). Validation gate: run it over the 8 historically-classified §5.2 tickets and require >=6/8 agreement with the frozen E1 labels. Documented alarm rule: trailing blocking-FP proxy >10% -> demote that criterion to advisory (precedent: T3/T10) - dogfood monitoring, not a config flag.

## Scope
- docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/ (instrumentation job + README documenting metrics, alarm rule, and cadence).
- Optionally a make target or documented command; NO gate/source behavior changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The instrumentation job is committed and re-runnable against the live store, emitting per-criterion trailing metrics documented in a README.
- [ ] Reproduction gate: the job classifies the 8 §5.2 tickets with >=6/8 agreement against the E1 frozen labels, recorded in the writeup.
- [ ] The alarm rule (trailing blocking-FP proxy >10% -> demote to advisory) and response playbook are documented in docs/plan-review-gate.md.
data.title: R7: standing gate-eval outcome instrumentation + dogfood FP monitoring
